subclassFeature.name: Heart Hunter
entries: When you select this subclass at tier 1, you learn how to gruesomely take the power from a slain foe to become all the mightier. When you or an ally slays a living creature of a CR equal to half your level or higher, if it has died within the last minute you can use an action to strike into its body, rip out the heart, and take a massive bite. For a number of hours equal to half your level you gain an expertise die on ability checks.

Whether or not you are able to consume the heart of a creature is determined by the DM, and finding the heart of some creatures requires an {@skill Investigation} or {@skill Medicine} check (DC 10 + half the creature's CR).

subclassFeature.name: Heart-Eater Major Effects
entries: From tier 1, Heart-Eater Berserkers have access to the following major effect options:

items: The triggering attack deals additional base damage equal to your level, and you recover the same amount of hit points. If you are raging, the added damage and healing is granted to all of your attacks for the remainder of your rage.

The triggering attack deals additional damage equal to triple your level, and you lose a number of hit points equal to your level. If you are raging, the added damage and hit point loss is granted to all of your attacks for the remainder of your rage.

subclassFeature.name: Bloodstriker
entries: At tier 2, you can tap into your fury to control the sanguine flow of a bloody combat. While you are raging, after you hit a bloodied living creature with a weapon attack, you can use a bonus action to do one of the following:

entries.name: Bloodtrack
entries: For the next hour you know the direction and distance to the creature as long as you are both on the same plane of existence.

entries.name: Crimson Deflect
entries: You capture the creature's blood in mid-air, forming a floating shield around you. Until the start of your next turn, your armour increases by an amount equal to half your proficiency bonus.
Once you have used this feature a number of times equal to half your proficiency bonus, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Heart Hunter Improvement
entries: Also at tier 2, your Heart Hunter feature now additionally grants you an expertise die on weapon damage rolls for its duration.

subclassFeature.name: Bloody Work
entries: At tier 3, you become bloodied at 75% of your maximum hit points instead of 50%. Additionally, while you are bloodied, your attacks deal additional base damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Heart Strike
entries: Starting at tier 4, you twist a strike at just the right moment to turn it into a devastating blow. When you hit with a melee weapon attack against a living creature, you can use your reaction to turn the attack into a critical hit. Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest. Alternatively, when you use your Heart Hunter feature, instead of gaining its usual benefits, you can choose to regain the use of this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Heart Hunter Improvement
entries: Also at tier 4, your Heart Hunter feature now additionally grants you an expertise die on saves.

subclassFeature.name: Hard Dying
entries: At tier 5, you grow far beyond the blood that flows through your veins. When you are reduced to 0 hit points while raging, you can use your reaction to remain conscious for up to 1 minute so long as you are not dead. You continue to resolve the effects of dying as usual and begin to track your negative hit points. You immediately die when your negative hit points reach half your hit point maximum. Once you have used this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest.
If you die during this effect your body fades away, and {@dice 1d4} hours later you reappear bloodied in the last place you took a long rest, and you cannot use this feature again for a week.

subclassFeature.name: Heart Hunter Improvement
entries: Also at tier 5, your Heart Hunter feature now additionally grants you an expertise die on armour attack rolls.
